name: stats
description: Report read-only workflow progress, duration, retry/loop count, and git metrics for a ticket (or all tickets)
argument-hint: "<ticket-id|all>"
user-invocable: true
allowed-tools: Bash, Read
---

> **Resolve the plugin root before running any script below.** A codex shell
> can inherit a stale, set-but-WRONG `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` from its launcher,
> so a bare `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-‚Ä¶}` fallback is NOT safe ‚Äî validate the
> value against this skill's own SKILL.md first. Set `SKILL_MD` to this
> file's absolute path (codex lists it next to this skill in your skills
> list; under Claude Code the env var is always correct and the fallback
> branch never runs):
>
> ```bash
> SKILL_MD="/absolute/path/to/this/SKILL.md"   # from your skills list
> if [ -f "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-/nonexistent}/skills/stats/SKILL.md" ] &&
>    [ "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/stats/SKILL.md" -ef "$SKILL_MD" ]; then
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT"                         # validated ‚Äî use as-is
> else
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$SKILL_MD")/../.." && pwd)" # stale/unset ‚Äî self-locate
> fi
> ```
>
> Wherever this skill writes `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, substitute the validated
> `$PLUGIN_ROOT` value (shells do NOT share variables between tool calls ‚Äî
> re-resolve or inline the resolved path in each command).

# Stats Command

Report read-only workflow statistics for a `/work` ticket. `/stats` is a thin
skill that shells out to the backing Node script
(`plugins/work/scripts/stats/stats.js`), which reads each ticket's
`.work-state.json` and the ticket worktree's git history. It never mutates the
filesystem or git.

## Usage

```
/stats <ticket-id>
/stats all
```

**Examples:**
- `/stats PROJ-856` ‚Äî full metrics for a single ticket
- `/stats 856` ‚Äî same (bare GitHub issue number is sanitized to `GH-856`)
- `/stats all` ‚Äî one compact row per ticket dir under `TASKS_BASE`

## What it reports

For a single ticket:
- **Step position** ‚Äî current step + index from `ALL_STEPS` (e.g. `implement (9/19)`),
  plus completed-vs-remaining step counts derived from `stepStatus`.
- **Run duration** ‚Äî whole-run elapsed time from `startTime` ‚Üí `lastUpdate`. Per-step
  breakdown renders `n/a` (no per-step timestamps are recorded).
- **Retry/loop count** ‚Äî number of check ‚Üí implement re-entries derived from
  `checkProgress` / the `errors` history.
- **Git metrics** ‚Äî commit count and lines added/removed/files changed for the ticket
  branch/worktree versus the base branch. Renders `n/a` when no worktree is present.
- **Tokens** ‚Äî `tokens: n/a (requires GH-311)` until per-step/per-agent token totals
  are available.

For `/stats all`:
- A compact aggregation table with one row per ticket dir under `TASKS_BASE`.

## Instructions

### Step 1: Resolve the target

The first argument is either a ticket id or the literal `all`. A bare GitHub
issue number (e.g. `856`) is sanitized to `GH-856` by the backing script.

### Step 2: Run the backing script

```bash
node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/stats/stats.js <ticket-id|all>
```

The script:
- Resolves `TASKS_BASE` / `WORKTREES_BASE` / `REPO_NAME` and the worktree path
  through `getConfig` (never ad-hoc `process.env`).
- Reads state via `loadState` / `getStatePath` and step order via `ALL_STEPS`.
- Renders every line through the shared `lib/report-format.js` renderer
  (`[PASS]/[WARN]/[FAIL]` status lines + indented `Key: value` metric blocks).

### Step 3: Relay the output

Print the script's output verbatim. Do not re-interpret or recompute the metrics.

## Error handling

The script never throws and never mutates state:
- Unknown / uninitialized ticket ‚Üí a single `[FAIL] no .work-state.json for <ticket>`
  line and a non-zero exit code.
- Corrupt `.work-state.json` ‚Üí `[FAIL] unreadable state` without throwing.

## Notes

- **Read-only by design** ‚Äî `/stats` performs no filesystem or git mutation.
- Config, state, and step order are read exclusively through `getConfig`,
  `loadState` / `getStatePath`, and `ALL_STEPS`.
